title: Regarding Margie

jokes.text: We do it without asking, and then they have to pay.
jokes.setup: Explaining their business model
jokes.punchline: It's an extortion racket presented as entrepreneurship
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy disguised as innocent childhood enterprise - classic Simpsons subversion

jokes.text: Answer me, or I'll drop ya like a bad cell phone plan!
jokes.setup: Angry confrontation threat
jokes.punchline: Modern tech reference in tough guy dialogue
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever modern update to threatening language but not particularly original

jokes.text: Sorry. This thing goes off on its own. Anyway, you were sayin'?
jokes.setup: Neighbor was making threats with what seemed like a weapon
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be a malfunctioning device, deflating the tension
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of tension and expectation subversion

jokes.text: That really classes the joint up.
jokes.setup: Simple address number painted on curb
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere belief this is classy home improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his low standards presented as genuine appreciation

jokes.text: I struck oil!
jokes.setup: Homer's excited exclamation about striking it rich
jokes.punchline: Visual reveals it's clearly not oil
jokes.explanation: Classic visual misdirection with Homer's delusional optimism

jokes.text: When do we tell him that's not oil? Let him have his fun.
jokes.setup: Homer's oil delusion continues
jokes.punchline: Kids' knowing commentary and decision to enable the delusion
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic humor - kids as the wise ones enabling dad's fantasy

jokes.text: Only ten dollars to paint my curb like a common whore?
jokes.setup: Ned commenting on the curb painting price
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly crude comparison from the religious neighbor
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character comedy - Ned's repressed nature emerging in inappropriate metaphor

jokes.text: That's not a scam! That's honest work for honest pay! No!
jokes.setup: Ned offers legitimate payment for more work
jokes.punchline: Bart's horror at the prospect of honest labor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - his moral compass pointing toward dishonesty

jokes.text: Well, then we're gonna leave you one digit short.
jokes.setup: Customer refuses to pay for curb painting
jokes.punchline: Threat to vandalize the address number
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the scam with specific, logical revenge

jokes.text: Visual gag: Homer paints elaborate '74 Oakland A's tribute instead of house number
jokes.setup: Homer was supposed to paint one number to complete the address
jokes.punchline: He creates an elaborate baseball tribute completely unrelated to the task
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer ADD moment - task completely derailed by nostalgic impulse

jokes.text: But, sweetie, I just wanted to tell the '74 Oakland A's how I felt about them.
jokes.setup: Lisa's frustration about the ruined house number
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere emotional motivation for the vandalism
jokes.explanation: Homer's genuine emotional connection to random nostalgia as justification

jokes.text: Look, that guy remembers us! Hey, sal bando, give him a '74 a's 'thank you honk.'
jokes.setup: Someone honks at their house
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks it's Sal Bando appreciating his tribute
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusions of celebrity acknowledgment

jokes.text: On second thought, I am Brenda Winecooler.
jokes.setup: Marge correctly states they're not the Weinpartens, delivery person mentions steaks
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal with hilariously wrong name variation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of moral compromise with the wrong name making it funnier

jokes.text: 74? Oh, sorry. There's been a mistake.
jokes.setup: Delivery for 74 Evergreen Terrace
jokes.punchline: Can't find the address because of Homer's mural
jokes.explanation: Direct consequence of Homer's actions creating practical problems

jokes.text: Son, all our mail has ever brought me is bad things: Bills, jury duty, Entertainment Weekly.
jokes.setup: Bart questions the ethics of mail theft
jokes.punchline: Homer's list escalates from reasonable complaints to petty magazine grievance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Entertainment Weekly as the absurd final complaint

jokes.text: Oh, dad, no one can rationalize like you.
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate justification for mail theft
jokes.punchline: Bart's admiring recognition of Homer's talent for self-deception
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on Homer's character trait, very quotable line

jokes.text: Mark and Tracy, I remember the first words I heard you say: 'I do.'
jokes.setup: Homer giving a wedding speech as if he knows the couple
jokes.punchline: He literally only heard them during the ceremony
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure revealing the absurdity of wedding crashers

jokes.text: That's what I heard, but he's so confident in his movements.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Scott being blind
jokes.punchline: Observation suggests he might not actually be blind
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection but not particularly strong or memorable

jokes.text: The lid from my coffee cupis long gone. I'm scared, people. Truly scared.
jokes.setup: Kent reporting from hurricane
jokes.punchline: Focus shifts from serious weather to trivial coffee cup lid
jokes.explanation: Good Kent Brockman characterization - dramatic about minor inconveniences

jokes.text: Take that, liberal media.
jokes.setup: News reporter struggling in hurricane conditions
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced political victory declaration
jokes.explanation: Homer's random political non-sequitur at inappropriate moment

jokes.text: Take that, liberal media.
jokes.setup: Watching news coverage of hurricane affecting reporter
jokes.punchline: Homer's political commentary on reporter's misfortune
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Homer's political outlook

jokes.text: Marge, I don't have to sit here and listen to your insane ravings about me getting other people's mail.
jokes.setup: Marge wondering how she won a contest she didn't enter
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant denial while literally doing exactly what she suspects
jokes.explanation: Good ironic denial from Homer while obviously guilty

jokes.text: Don't be so naive, Lisa. Those people all talk to each other.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions why Marge cares what a professional housekeeper thinks
jokes.punchline: Marge's paranoid belief in a housekeeper gossip network
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's social anxiety and paranoia

jokes.text: Not a fine upstanding head hair, but a curly-wurly!
jokes.setup: Professional housekeeper finding hair in drain
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemism for pubic hair combined with professional disgust
jokes.explanation: Memorable euphemism that's both childish and gross, very quotable

jokes.text: I don't want that judgmental bitch in my house!
jokes.setup: Marge has been anxiously preparing for the housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Her anxiety transforms into aggressive hostility
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation - Marge's rare profanity showing her stress breaking point

jokes.text: Et tu, Zud?
jokes.setup: Housekeeper overwhelmed by cleaning chemicals
jokes.punchline: Shakespeare reference with cleaning product brand name
jokes.explanation: Clever high-low culture collision with Julius Caesar and cleaning products

jokes.text: My head feels like it was punched by a boxing man.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her post-amnesia headache
jokes.punchline: Childlike, redundant description of being punched by a boxer
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent, childlike speech pattern post-amnesia

jokes.text: Now I'll put them back in the jar.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking Marge to count fingers for neurological test
jokes.punchline: Reveals the fingers are severed and stored in a jar
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark subversion of standard medical test with macabre twist

jokes.text: until your amnesia goes scram-nesia
jokes.setup: Homer promising to care for Marge during recovery
jokes.punchline: Made-up rhyming word that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic attempt at clever wordplay that doesn't work logically

jokes.text: You're sweet. I'm lucky to have such a kind... uncle?
jokes.setup: Marge appreciating Homer's kindness
jokes.punchline: She misidentifies their relationship as uncle/niece
jokes.explanation: Standard amnesia comedy trope but executed competently

jokes.text: But you're so much more... ravaged than I am.
jokes.setup: Homer insisting they're the same age
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal honest assessment of Homer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty from amnesia removing social filters, very quotable

jokes.text: I'm Lisa, I'm eight years old, and this might sound presumptuous, but... I'm your favorite.
jokes.setup: Lisa introducing herself to amnesiac Marge
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares herself the favorite child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - using intelligence and directness to gain advantage

jokes.text: I'm your favorite. You seem sweet, but I could never have a favorite.
jokes.setup: Lisa confidently declaring she's the favorite child
jokes.punchline: Marge's diplomatic rejection of the concept
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Lisa's confidence met with Marge's maternal diplomacy

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Bart. I'm ten years old and a professional motocross jumper. Can you buy me a motocross bike?
jokes.setup: Bart introducing himself to amnesiac mother
jokes.punchline: Immediately lies about profession to get expensive gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart opportunism - exploiting mother's vulnerability immediately

jokes.text: I may have lost my memory, but I'm not an idiot.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to scam his amnesiac mother
jokes.punchline: Marge's clear-eyed recognition of the obvious scam
jokes.explanation: Great comeback line showing amnesia doesn't equal stupidity, highly quotable

jokes.text: Like somebody barfed a two-story pile of puke.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about their home
jokes.punchline: Bart's graphic, redundant description of their house
jokes.explanation: Somewhat crude and redundant, relies on gross-out rather than cleverness

jokes.text: You strangle your child? Yeah, but... he's cool with it. Right? It hurts when I swallow.
jokes.setup: Marge horrified by Homer strangling Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual justification and Bart's deadpan injury report
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy examining the show's running gag through outsider eyes

jokes.text: Maraschino isn't even a kind of cherry! It's a way of preserving them!
jokes.setup: Argument about cherry types
jokes.punchline: Apu's pedantic correction about cherry preservation methods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment - pedantic food knowledge in heated debate

jokes.text: It goes by many names. Apollo's lantern, day-moon, old blazey.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the sun to amnesiac Marge
jokes.punchline: Made-up ridiculous alternate names for the sun
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pseudo-wisdom with absurd made-up names, highly quotable

jokes.text: The important thing is never to touch it.
jokes.setup: Homer's sun education continues
jokes.punchline: Completely obvious advice delivered as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of completely obvious advice as if it's wisdom

jokes.text: I know what the sun is! Yes, now you do.
jokes.setup: Homer's condescending explanation
jokes.punchline: Homer taking credit for Marge's obvious knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ego moment - taking credit for basic knowledge

jokes.text: The sound of the baby must have triggered a primal mnemonic response in mom's brain!
jokes.setup: Marge suddenly remembering Maggie
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly scientific explanation for maternal instinct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - overcomplicating simple emotional connection

jokes.text: Hey, guys, guess who just became lactose-tolerant?
jokes.setup: Emotional reunion between Marge and her children
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's completely unrelated medical update
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - inappropriate oversharing at emotional moment

jokes.text: Who's got amnesia now, Bart?
jokes.setup: Bart upset that Marge remembered him through Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's nonsensical victory declaration
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's illogical attempt at a comeback that makes no sense

jokes.text: All right, Marge, time to remember admiral awesome!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to jog Marge's memory of him
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous self-aggrandizing nickname
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional self-image in desperate attempt to seem memorable

jokes.text: I don't know, maybe there's something unpleasant blocking my memory of you.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering why she remembers others but not him
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - amnesia allows honest assessment of marriage

jokes.text: Oh, hey, ned. Isn't today your cat's birthday? I remember, 'cause she shares the date with Patty LaBelle.
jokes.setup: Marge can't remember Homer
jokes.punchline: But remembers obscure detail about neighbor's cat
jokes.explanation: Great ironic contrast - remembers trivial details but not husband

jokes.text: Here's me fighting with former president Bush. Me fighting with current president Bush.
jokes.setup: Homer showing photos to trigger Marge's memory
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's fought with multiple presidents
jokes.explanation: Good callback to show's history of Homer vs. authority figures

jokes.text: You're describing how to parallel park.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to describe intimate activities euphemistically
jokes.punchline: His euphemisms are so vague they describe mundane activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect setup and punchline structure

jokes.text: You're describing how to parallel park.
jokes.setup: Homer's awkward romantic explanation
jokes.punchline: Marge's deadpan observation about his technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant punchline - perfect metaphor for Homer's romantic incompetence

jokes.text: You used to love my non sequiturs.
jokes.setup: Homer's failed romantic explanation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about his communication style
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke - Homer showing unexpected self-awareness

jokes.text: Simple. Your brain is trying to save you from your backed-up toilet of a marriage.
jokes.setup: Marge wondering why she can't remember Homer
jokes.punchline: Patty's brutal metaphor for their marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty character moment - hostile metaphor with memorable imagery

jokes.text: Yeah, and you said you're only 180 pounds. Shut up and look for crime.
jokes.setup: Police hang-gliding experiment gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Weight limit exceeded, followed by maintaining the facade
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy setup with character-appropriate incompetence

jokes.text: That Homer Simpson he's quite a guy / he paid me ten bucks so I would lie / the truth is he's not so great / he is the one man I truly hate.
jokes.setup: Romantic serenade supposedly about Homer's greatness
jokes.punchline: Song reveals it's paid performance and expresses actual hatred
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic gesture with escalating brutal honesty, very memorable

jokes.text: So beautiful. I wish I could understand italian.
jokes.setup: Tony Bennett singing insulting song about Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's complete misunderstanding of what she heard
jokes.explanation: Good ironic misunderstanding - Marge appreciates the melody not the message

jokes.text: No! I'm not the kind of girl who makes love on a putt-putt golf course!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to recreate their romantic history
jokes.punchline: Reveals their 'romantic' venue was completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy revealing the tackiness of their romantic history

jokes.text: You loved it, especially when the windmill came around and whacked your butt.
jokes.setup: Marge's horror at mini-golf romance
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious addition of embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Homer's romantic obliviousness - sharing TMI details

jokes.text: The best thing that ever happened to me was forgetting about you!
jokes.setup: Homer's desperate romantic gestures failing
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal declaration that amnesia improved her life
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional climax with brutal honesty from Marge

jokes.text: Birthday's over, Seymour.
jokes.setup: Skinner being kicked out
jokes.punchline: Agnes's cold dismissal of her adult son
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes - cold efficiency in family cruelty

jokes.text: How long you gonna be bunking with me, son? Well, the rest of your life for sure.
jokes.setup: Homer moving back with his father
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact acceptance of permanent arrangement
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic acceptance of permanent failure and dependence

jokes.text: Every five minutes I'm going to blow this horn... And each man will move one table to his left.
jokes.setup: Speed dating explanation
jokes.punchline: Mechanical, dehumanizing description of romance
jokes.explanation: Standard commentary on speed dating's mechanical nature

jokes.text: I don't drive a lexus, okay? I don't have a lear jet or some big beach house in la-la land. I am just a nice man who makes a decent living. So I guess I should probably just go shoot myself?
jokes.setup: Man explaining his modest lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation to suicide over being middle-class
jokes.explanation: Self-pity escalation to absurd dramatic conclusion

jokes.text: Don't get quiet on me. Please, I hate when they get quiet.
jokes.setup: Aggressive speed dater overwhelming Marge with negativity
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea revealing this is a pattern
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing dating desperation and self-awareness

jokes.text: We used to be quintuplets, but three of us drowned.
jokes.setup: Man explaining he was dragged there by brothers
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of siblings' deaths
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - tragic backstory delivered casually

jokes.text: We used to be quintuplets, but three of us drowned.
jokes.setup: Shy man explaining he was dragged there by his brothers
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of massive family tragedy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with casual delivery of shocking information

jokes.text: But it's not an awkward silence. Yeah, it's more like a nothing needs to be said kind of silence.
jokes.setup: Lenny and Carl reporting on Marge's date to Homer
jokes.punchline: Their detailed analysis of the romantic silence types
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about different types of silence with painful accuracy

jokes.text: Oh, no! He's Marge's and my dream man.
jokes.setup: Watching Marge connect with new guy
jokes.punchline: Homer's admission of shared attraction
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest self-awareness about his own inadequacy

jokes.text: The wisest, wettest man you'll ever meet.
jokes.setup: Aquaman introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Combining wisdom with his water-based powers awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Aquaman's attempt at impressive self-description fails

jokes.text: The wisest, wettest man you'll ever meet.
jokes.setup: Willie confronting the man who rejected Marge
jokes.punchline: His strange self-description combining wisdom with dampness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment - mysterious wisdom combined with his perpetual dampness

jokes.text: In ten years, she never had the last slice of pizza and she's never complained. Every election she wishes she could vote for both guys because they both seem nice.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Marge to the speed dating guy
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of her selflessness and naivety
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny character description - perfect Marge details

jokes.text: You drink? Yeah, I started when you were in the hospital. I was so worried. Recently I tried this thing called beer, and...
jokes.setup: Marge asking about Homer's drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent explanation of recent beer discovery
jokes.explanation: Homer's touching but misguided way of dealing with stress

jokes.text: I remember everything! You get drunk all the time. But you also remember that you're an enabler? Of course I do. That's why we're such a great team.
jokes.setup: Marge's memory returning
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition of their dysfunctional but functional dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - cynical but loving assessment of their marriage

jokes.text: But you also remember that you're an enabler? Of course I do. That's why we're such a great team.
jokes.setup: Marge remembers Homer's drinking problem
jokes.punchline: She also remembers and embraces her role as enabler
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark ending - their dysfunctional relationship as a perfect partnership, very quotable

tmdb_overview: Marge goes on a cleaning frenzy before the housekeeper she’s won the services of comes to the house. One spot remains and she mixes a number of toxic cleaners together to tackle the job. The noxious mixture knocks her out and she wakes up suffering from amnesia. Marge is taken home and the family tries to bring back her memory. She starts to get back memories of her children and other insignificant things, but when it comes to remembering her husband, nothing he does seems to jog her memory. When he can’t get her to remember him, he tries to win her all over again, especially after Patty and Selma get her back out on the dating scene.
